negative_variants.description: Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Integrate branch current to determine the contributed branch voltage.

Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use voltage contribution with idt() of branch current for an inductor-style model fail.
Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Integrate branch current to determine the contributed branch voltage fails.

Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use voltage contribution with idt() of branch current for an inductor-style model fail.
